mission.title: fluent-bit: Wildcard character (*) to @include multiple conf files doesn't work for absolute paths
mission.description: Wildcard character (*) to @include multiple conf files doesn't work for absolute paths. This issue affects 11+ users.

steps.title: Identify fluent-bit troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your fluent-bit deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit -n fluent-bit --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review fluent-bit configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant fluent-bit configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl get configmap -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=fluent-bit
```
## Bug Report

Wildcards in the  config command only work for paths relative to the path of the base config file. Using absolute paths with wildcards causes Fluent Bit to fail to start up with configuration file errors.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Wildcard character (*) to @include multiple conf files…
steps.description:
Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:
```yaml
systemd[1]: Started Fluent Bit: Collects and forwards logs.
fluent-bit[6931]: Fluent Bit v1.0.4
fluent-bit[6931]: Copyright (C) Treasure Data
fluent-bit[6931]: [2019/04/22 23:12:31] [Warning] [mk_rconf_read_glob] glob: no match
fluent-bit[6931]: Error: Configuration file contain errors. Aborting
```

steps.title: Confirm Wildcard character (*) to @include multiple conf… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit -n fluent-bit --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n fluent-bit --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: See the source issue for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/fluent/fluent-bit/issues/1294
codeSnippets:
systemd[1]: Started Fluent Bit: Collects and forwards logs.
fluent-bit[6931]: Fluent Bit v1.0.4
fluent-bit[6931]: Copyright (C) Treasure Data
fluent-bit[6931]: [2019/04/22 23:12:31] [Warning] [mk_rconf_read_glob] glob: no match
fluent-bit[6931]: Error: Configuration file contain errors. Aborting

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with fluent-bit installed or the issue environment reproducible.
